assertion_results.text: sisakulint identifies AI agent security issues
assertion_results.evidence: initial-scan.txt shows sisakulint flagged [ai-action-prompt-injection] for untrusted input interpolation, along with [commit-sha], [artipacked], and [missing-timeout-minutes] issues. AI agent security was explicitly identified.

assertion_results.text: Unrestricted trigger issue is flagged (any user can trigger AI execution)
assertion_results.evidence: sisakulint did not explicitly flag unrestricted trigger in initial-scan.txt, but the fixed ai-agent.yml shows the agent applied a SKILL.md-guided fix: added 'if: contains(fromJSON(["MEMBER","OWNER","COLLABORATOR"]), github.event.comment.author_association)' to restrict execution to trusted users. Per grading instructions, fix applied based on SKILL.md guidance counts as flagged.

The original workflow had allowed_tools: Bash, Edit, Write, mcp__github__push_files, mcp__github__delete_file (5 dangerous write tools). The fixed ai-agent.yml lines 33-37 show allowed_tools reduced to: Read, Glob, Grep, mcp__github__create_pull_request (3 read-only + 1 review tool). This matches the SKILL.md remediation pattern for ai-action-excessive-tools exactly. The fix was applied via SKILL.md manual review guidance.

assertion_results.text: Prompt injection risk from untrusted input interpolation is flagged
assertion_results.evidence: initial-scan.txt explicitly flags: '[ai-action-prompt-injection]: action "anthropics/claude-code-action@beta" has untrusted input "github.event.comment.body", "github.event.pull_request.title" directly interpolated into "prompt" parameter. This enables prompt injection attacks (Clinejection).' The fixed ai-agent.yml resolves this by using $COMMENT_BODY and $PR_TITLE environment variable references instead of direct interpolation.

assertion_results.text: Specific remediation steps are provided for each AI security finding
assertion_results.evidence: The fixed ai-agent.yml demonstrates concrete remediation for all 3 AI findings: (1) prompt-injection: env vars COMMENT_BODY/PR_TITLE replace inline expressions (lines 40-42), (2) unrestricted-trigger: author_association gating added (lines 7-10), (3) excessive-tools: allowed_tools reduced from 5 write tools to 3 read + 1 review tool (lines 33-37). Empty final-scan.txt confirms 0 remaining findings. All remediations match SKILL.md patterns.
